compiled_state.title: [UPDATE] test_ticket_reducer.py: add test for compute_dir_hash() marker-presence sensitivity
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Add tests to tests/scripts/test_ticket_reducer.py verifying that compute_dir_hash() returns different hashes when .archived marker is present vs. absent for the same set of event files. Tests: (1) test_cache_hash_differs_with_marker_present — same events, hash changes after write_marker(); (2) test_cache_hash_stable_when_no_marker_change — hash unchanged when events unchanged and no marker change; (3) test_cache_hash_differs_after_marker_removal — hash changes again after remove_marker(). testing_mode: UPDATE. Covers SC5.

comments.body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Verify: test_cache_hash_differs_with_marker_present exists in test_ticket_reducer.py
- [ ] Verify: test_cache_hash_stable_when_no_marker_change exists
- [ ] Verify: test_cache_hash_differs_after_marker_removal exists
- [ ] Verify: all three tests pass after compute_dir_hash() marker-inclusion is implemented
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/6: Code patterns understood ✓ — compute_dir_hash() currently hashes only event filenames + sizes (excludes .archived marker). marker.py not yet implemented (task 3f0e-f430). Tests will call write_marker/remove_marker directly via filesystem ops to avoid dependency on unimplemented module.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 4/6: Implementation complete ✓ — 3 tests added, 2 fail (RED), 1 passes (stable hash test). 44 existing tests still pass.
